_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites 'text' from live numbers; these are the computed fallbacks so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.cape: The Shiller CAPE ratio stands at 40.7 in June 2026, more than double its 140-year average of 17.3 and nearing the dot-com bubble peak of 44.19 from December 1999, suggesting valuations may be stretched. Such a high CAPE warns that stocks might be priced for unusually strong future earnings, raising bubble concerns.
notes.buffett: At 231.7%, the Buffett Indicator, which compares total market capitalization to GDP, is vastly above previous peaks like 146% during the dot-com boom and 110% before the 2007 crash. This extreme ratio signals the market is valued well beyond historical norms relative to economic output, a classic bubble signal.
notes.concentration: Market concentration is high, with the top 5 companies making up nearly 30% of the S&P 500 and tech stocks comprising 48% of the index. The top 10 stocks alone hold 41% of the market in 2026, up from just 17.5% in 2015, indicating a narrow leadership that can amplify risks if sentiment shifts.
notes.yieldcurve: The yield curve spread between 10-year and 3-month Treasury yields is 0.55 percentage points, indicating a mildly positive slope but with the New York Fed assigning a 31% probability of recession. This suggests economic caution amid the frothy market, as yield curves often anticipate downturns.
notes.drypowder: Record cash reserves sit with Berkshire Hathaway at $397.4 billion in Q1 2026, signaling plenty of dry powder—capital ready for investment. Yet, 79% of institutions expect their cash holdings to decline, implying future liquidity might tighten just as valuations peak.
notes.finale: The IPO lock-up expirations between December 2026 and May 2027, involving giants like SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic, could flood the market with new shares. Combined with the expected decline in institutional cash, this window may drain liquidity and pressure inflated valuations, testing the market's endurance.
